content.thinking:
Let me analyze the three grids:

Target grid:
- Has filled cells (X) in columns 4 and 5 (the rightmost two columns)
- Has empty cells (в–Ў) in columns 1, 2, and 3 (the leftmost three columns)
- This pattern is consistent across all 5 rows

Distractor grid 1:
- Has filled cells (X) in columns 3, 4, and 5 (the rightmost three columns)
- Has empty cells (в–Ў) in columns 1 and 2 (the leftmost two columns)
- This pattern is consistent across all 5 rows

Distractor grid 2:
- Has filled cells (X) in columns 1, 4, and 5
- Has empty cells (в–Ў) in columns 2 and 3
- This pattern is consistent across all 5 rows

Key distinguishing features of the target:
- It has exactly 2 columns of filled cells (the rightmost 2)
- It has exactly 3 columns of empty cells (the leftmost 3)
- The filled cells form a vertical block on the right side
- Distractor 1 has 3 filled columns on the right
- Distractor 2 has filled cells on both left and right edges

Best description: "Two vertical columns of filled cells on the right side" or "Three empty columns on the left, two filled columns on the right"

content.text: Expression: Two vertical columns of filled cells aligned to the right edge
